title: Delegated Morty's submit ticket completions without files_changed metadata, triggering quality-gate rejections
source_context: Affected tickets: RICK-017 (orchestrator core), RICK-018 (codex shim), RICK-019 (adjudicator). All three landed in in_review with rejector message: 'missing files_changed or description'. Real code may still be on disk in rick-terminal/src/main/counsel/ — needs manual inspection.
description: Observed in RickIDE sprint 2026-06-05. Backend-morty (RICK-017, 018) and architect-morty (RICK-019) closed their tickets via delegate.py wrapper, but the submitted output object lacks 'files_changed' and/or 'description' fields. Reviewer-morty quality-gate rejects on this metadata absence, even when actual code exists in the repo. Either: (1) the delegate.py post-execution hook is not collecting git diff output into files_changed, or (2) the Morty's system prompt doesn't instruct them to emit a structured completion summary that the wrapper can parse.
proposed_changes: After Morty subprocess exits, capture git status --porcelain + git diff --stat from the cwd into files_changed automatically. Don't trust the Morty to self-report — derive metadata from filesystem reality. Also: prompt template should ask Morty to emit a final '## Files changed' markdown block as a backup signal.
